content.problemStatement:
You are given a permutation array AAA of integers in the range [1,N][1, N][1,N].

Your task is to handle QQQ queries of the following type:


Given LLL and RRR, first create a new array BBB, also of length NNN, according to the following:

Bi=LB_i = LBi​=L if Ai<LA_i < LAi​<L.
Bi=AiB_i = A_iBi​=Ai​ if L≤Ai≤RL \le A_i \le RL≤Ai​≤R
Bi=RB_i = RBi​=R if R<AiR < A_iR<Ai​.


Find the number of inversions†^{\dagger}† in BBB.
†^{\dagger}† An inversion is a pair (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that 1≤i<j≤N1 \le i < j \le N1≤i<j≤N and Bi>BjB_i > B_jBi​>Bj​.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN and QQQ - the size of the array and the number of queries.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, the array.
The next QQQ lines contain 222 integers each - LLL and RRR, the parameters for each query.




Output Format
For each query, output on a new line the number of inversions in the constructed array BBB.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.
The sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.
